purpose: Generic executable Verilog-A mechanism skeletons distilled from validated R26/closure mechanisms. These are not task-specific answers; prompts must bind slots to public ports, parameters, and observables.

skeletons.title: DWA Pointer / Window / Enable Skeleton

slot_schema.reset: optional public reset input and polarity
slot_schema.code_bits: ordered public input bits or scalar code input
slot_schema.ptr_outputs: scalar pointer outputs or bus bits
slot_schema.cell_outputs: scalar cell-enable outputs or bus bits

implementation_skeleton: Declare integer pointer state, current code, previous/window code, loop index, and modulo index at module scope.
Declare real target arrays for pointer outputs and cell-enable outputs at module scope.
Inside @(initial_step), normalize pointer into [0,N-1], clear all target arrays, and set the held pointer one-hot target.
Inside the requested clock event, first decode the public code bits using fixed-index voltage reads and clamp the active count.
If reset is active, restore pointer and clear masks. Otherwise advance the pointer once per valid event according to the no-overlap/wrap rule.
Clear every target array entry, set exactly one pointer target, then construct the complete selected cell window using modulo arithmetic before any output contribution.
Drive every electrical output from its held target with unconditional transition() contributions outside the event block.
veriloga_shape: integer ptr_q, code_q, prev_code_q, j, idx;
real cell_target[0:N-1]; real ptr_target[0:N-1]; genvar k;
@(initial_step) begin clear_targets(); ptr_q = normalized_init; ptr_target[ptr_q] = vhi; end
if (reset_active) begin ptr_q = normalized_init; code_q = 0; prev_code_q = 0; end
else begin ptr_q = next_pointer(ptr_q, prev_code_q, N); prev_code_q = code_q; end
for (j=0; j<code_q; j=j+1) begin idx = (ptr_q + j) % N; cell_target[idx] = vhi; end
for (k=0; k<N; k=k+1) begin V(cell_outputs[k]) <+ transition(cell_target[k],0,tr,tr); V(ptr_outputs[k]) <+ transition(ptr_target[k],0,tr,tr); end
anti_patterns: Do not toggle cell enables independently of pointer and decoded code.
Do not put transition() contributions inside runtime if/case branches.
Do not repair by changing only save names or aliases.

skeletons.title: ADC Quantize / DAC Reconstruct Skeleton

slot_schema.clock: sample clock when the prompt requests sampled ADC behavior
slot_schema.code_outputs: public code-bit outputs or scalar code observable

implementation_skeleton: Use one held integer code as the source of truth for both public code bits and DAC reconstruction.
On reset, set the held code and output target to the public reset state.
On the requested sample event, compute vstep from public reference rails, quantize vin by floor/truncation, and clamp to [0, 2^width-1].
Immediately update all code-bit targets from that same held code using fixed bit extraction.
Compute the DAC target from the same held code and reference range. Do not let vout track raw vin continuously when the prompt asks for quantized reconstruction.
Drive code bits and vout with unconditional transition() contributions from held targets.
veriloga_shape: real bit_target[0:WIDTH-1]; real vout_target; real vref_span, vstep;
@(initial_step) begin code_q = 0; vout_target = vss; clear_bit_targets(); end
else begin vref_span = V(vdd)-V(vss); vstep = vref_span/(1<<WIDTH); code_q = floor((V(vin)-V(vss))/vstep); code_q = clamp(code_q,0,max_code); end
for (b=0; b<WIDTH; b=b+1) bit_target[b] = bit_is_set(code_q,b) ? vhi : vlo;
anti_patterns: Do not compute code bits and vout from different state variables.
Do not use dynamic electrical bus indexing if Spectre-compatible scalar outputs are available.
Do not hard-code reference scale when the prompt exposes VDD/VSS or VREF.

skeletons.title: PFD Edge-Order / Non-Overlap Pulse Skeleton

slot_schema.pulse_width: public pulse width or reset delay parameter

implementation_skeleton: Keep held real targets for UP and DN outputs.
On REF leading edge, assert UP target and schedule its clear time; on DIV leading edge, assert DN target and schedule its clear time.
If the opposite pulse is active, clear it or block overlap according to the prompt's reset-race rule.
Use timer events or explicit clear deadlines to deassert pulses after the public width.
Drive UP and DN with unconditional transition() contributions; never drive both high in the same stable window.
veriloga_shape: real up_target, dn_target, up_clear_t, dn_clear_t; integer up_active, dn_active;
@(initial_step) begin up_target=0; dn_target=0; up_active=0; dn_active=0; end
@(cross(V(ref)-vth,+1)) begin if (!dn_active) begin up_active=1; up_target=vhi; up_clear_t=$abstime+pulse_width; end end
@(cross(V(div)-vth,+1)) begin if (!up_active) begin dn_active=1; dn_target=vhi; dn_clear_t=$abstime+pulse_width; end end
anti_patterns: Do not infer UP/DN from a static phase sign only; the checker usually expects edge-triggered pulse windows.
Do not let UP and DN overlap except if the prompt explicitly requests overlap measurement.
Do not omit the clear timer; stuck-high pulses fail window checkers.

skeletons.title: PLL Feedback Divider / Lock Cadence Skeleton
implementation_skeleton: Use one oscillator phase/timer state as the source of truth for DCO edges.
Toggle feedback only by counting oscillator edges up to the public divider ratio.
Update control state from ref/fb edge timing error, then derive oscillator period/frequency from that same control state.
Assert lock only after a stable late-window relation between ref edges and feedback edges; drop lock on ratio hop or frequency step until the relation settles again.
Expose vctrl/lock/fb from held targets with unconditional transition() contributions.
veriloga_shape: integer div_count, stable_count, lock_q; real dco_target, fb_target, lock_target, vctrl_q, next_dco_t, dco_period;
@(initial_step) begin initialize phase, divider, vctrl, lock, and next_dco_t; end
if (rising_dco_edge) begin div_count = div_count + 1; if (div_count >= div_ratio) begin div_count=0; fb_target=toggle(fb_target); end end
@(cross(V(ref_clk)-vth,+1)) begin update phase/frequency error using latest fb edge; update vctrl_q; recompute dco_period; update stable_count and lock_q; end
V(fb_clk) <+ transition(fb_target,0,tr,tr); V(lock) <+ transition(lock_q ? vhi : vlo,0,tr,tr); V(vctrl) <+ transition(vctrl_q,0,tr,tr);
anti_patterns: Do not generate fb_clk as an independent free-running timer unrelated to the oscillator/divider state.
Do not assert lock permanently from time zero.
Do not make vctrl a cosmetic ramp disconnected from DCO cadence.

skeletons.title: Clock Divider / Counter Ratio Skeleton
skeletons.source: closed-set ledger: counter_or_divider_sequence residuals; docs/CONTRACT_REPAIR_CARDS.json divider/counter cards
implementation_skeleton: Keep an integer edge counter and held output target.
On reset, clear the counter and set output to the public reset state.
On each input clock edge, increment the counter; when it reaches the active ratio, clear it and toggle the held output target.
For runtime ratio changes, sample the ratio at a safe input edge and apply it to subsequent periods.
Drive the output with one unconditional transition() contribution outside event bodies.
veriloga_shape: @(cross(V(clock)-vth,+1)) begin if (reset_active) begin count_q=0; out_q=0; end else begin ratio_q=read_ratio(); count_q=count_q+1; if (count_q>=ratio_q) begin count_q=0; out_q=!out_q; end end end
anti_patterns: Do not use an independent free-running timer when the prompt requires input-edge division.
Do not ignore runtime ratio-code changes.
Do not put transition() inside the edge event.

skeletons.title: Comparator Threshold / Hysteresis Skeleton
skeletons.source: closed-set ledger: comparator_threshold_hysteresis residuals; comparator repair cards
implementation_skeleton: Keep one held integer decision state.
For simple comparators, set the state according to input minus threshold or vinp-vinn.
For hysteresis, use two thresholds: upper threshold for low-to-high and lower threshold for high-to-low.
Use cross events or continuous decision updates according to the prompt timing requirement.
Drive the output from the held state with an unconditional transition() contribution.
veriloga_shape: if (state_q==0 && diff > vhys/2) state_q=1; else if (state_q==1 && diff < -vhys/2) state_q=0;
anti_patterns: Do not reverse comparator polarity unless the prompt explicitly asks for inversion.
Do not make hysteresis a cosmetic parameter that is unused in the decision.
Do not leave output as an ideal discontinuous assignment when transition smoothing is required.

skeletons.title: DAC Binary / Thermometer / Segmented Decode Skeleton
skeletons.source: closed-set ledger: converter tasks; negative retrieval guard for binary-vs-thermometer

slot_schema.code_inputs: binary bits, thermometer cells, or segmented MSB/LSB groups
slot_schema.width: code width or number of unit cells

implementation_skeleton: First classify the code style from the prompt: binary-weighted, thermometer/unary, segmented, or DWA unit-cell window.
Decode the public inputs into one held integer code or active-count value.
For binary DACs, use weighted bit positions; for thermometer DACs, count active cells; for segmented DACs, combine MSB thermometer and LSB binary parts.
Compute vout from the decoded value and public reference range.
Drive vout with an unconditional transition() contribution.
anti_patterns: Do not use thermometer decoding for a prompt that explicitly says binary-weighted or not thermometer.
Do not assume monotonic ordering if the prompt explicitly allows arbitrary code order.
Do not compute output from a different state than the decoded public code.

skeletons.title: Sample-Hold Track / Latch / Droop Skeleton
skeletons.source: closed-set ledger: sample_hold_track_latch residuals; sample-hold skill/card knowledge

slot_schema.droop: optional droop rate or leakage behavior
slot_schema.vhi_vlo_vth: control threshold and output rails if digital

implementation_skeleton: Keep one held real sample value.
During track mode, update the held value from the input if the prompt requests tracking; otherwise update only on the sample edge.
During hold mode, preserve the held value and optionally apply the public droop model.
Drive output from the held value with an unconditional contribution/transition according to output type.
anti_patterns: Do not continuously buffer input when the prompt expects hold behavior.
Do not sample on the wrong edge.
Do not implement droop as an unrelated ramp disconnected from the held sample.

skeletons.title: LFSR / PRBS Sequence Skeleton
skeletons.source: closed-set ledger: lfsr_prbs_sequence residuals; PRBS/LFSR verified artifacts

slot_schema.polynomial: tap positions or named PRBS length

implementation_skeleton: Keep one integer shift-register state.
Initialize to a nonzero seed unless the prompt requires a specific seed.
On each clock edge, compute feedback from fixed taps, shift state, and update held output targets.
Expose serial/state bits from the same held state with unconditional transition() contributions.
veriloga_shape: @(cross(V(clock)-vth,+1)) begin fb = xor_taps(state_q); state_q = next_lfsr_state(state_q, fb); end
anti_patterns: Do not use all-zero LFSR seed unless explicitly requested.
Do not replace PRBS with a simple counter.
Do not update output from a different state than the shifted register.

skeletons.title: Calibration Search / Code Update / Settled Flag Skeleton
skeletons.source: closed-set ledger: calibration_search_settle residuals; calibration repair cards
implementation_skeleton: Keep one integer calibration/trim code as the source of truth.
Update the code from the public metric or comparator result according to the requested search rule.
Drive every code output from that held code using fixed bit positions.
Assert settled only after the metric/code remains stable for the requested window.
Drive code and settled outputs with unconditional transition() contributions.
veriloga_shape: @(cross(V(clock)-vth,+1)) begin update_code_from_metric(); update_stable_count(); settled_q = stable_count >= required_count; end
for (b=0; b<WIDTH; b=b+1) V(code_outputs[b]) <+ transition(bit_value(code_q,b) ? vhi : vlo,0,tr,tr);
anti_patterns: Do not assert settled from time zero.
Do not drive code outputs independently of the held calibration code.
Do not replace search behavior with a fixed constant unless the prompt explicitly asks for a fixed code.

skeletons.title: Serializer Frame / Bit Order / Alignment Skeleton

slot_schema.parallel_data: parallel input data or generated frame pattern

implementation_skeleton: Keep a held shift register and bit index.
Load the parallel/frame word at the requested frame boundary.
On each bit clock, drive the selected bit according to public bit order and advance the index.
Update frame/valid outputs from the same frame state.
Drive serial and marker outputs with unconditional transition() contributions.
anti_patterns: Do not ignore bit order.
Do not free-run the serial pattern without loading the public frame data.
Do not let frame marker drift independently of bit index.
